designSystem.name: BreadCo Production Console
designSystem.description: Admin/POS-style design system for a multi-branch bakery production management system. Clean, data-dense, register/dashboard aesthetic in deep navy blue and white. Use this file as the single source of truth when generating any new page or component for this system.
vibe: POS / register console, not a customer-facing storefront
Deep navy blue + white, minimal accent color
Numbers and IDs are visually distinct from labels (monospace)
Status communicated through color-coded pills/dots, not icons alone

usageRules: blue.950 and blue.900 are for the sidebar background gradient and the darkest text on white surfaces.
blue.700/600 are the primary interactive accent: active nav state text, links, primary buttons, active pipeline nodes.
blue.500 is for secondary accents and borders on active elements.
blue.100/50 are light tints for icon chips, selected backgrounds, and badge backgrounds on white surfaces. Never used as large background fills.
White (#FFFFFF) is the dominant surface color for all cards, panels, tables, and the main content area background.
ink is default body/heading text on white surfaces.
slate600/slate400 are for secondary text, captions, timestamps, placeholder/idle states.
success/warning/danger are reserved ONLY for status meaning (stock alerts, order status, delta indicators) — never used decoratively.
Never introduce a new hue (no green/purple/orange backgrounds) outside the status colors above.
display (Space Grotesk, 600-700 weight) is used ONLY for: page titles (h1), panel titles, brand/logo text. Never for body copy or table cells.
body (Inter) is the default for all UI text: nav items, labels, table cells, descriptions, buttons.

mono (IBM Plex Mono, 500-600 weight) is used ONLY for data that reads like a register/receipt: KPI big numbers, order IDs, timestamps/ETAs, station counts, the clock in the topbar. This is a signature rule of the system — any numeric or ID value gets mono.

layout.structure: Two-column app shell: fixed-width sidebar (left) + fluid main content (right).

sections: sidebar footer: user avatar + name + role, pinned to bottom
topbar (page title + subtitle, left; branch selector + clock + icon buttons + avatar, right)
KPI row: 4-column grid of metric cards
primary panel: full-width feature panel (e.g. production pipeline tracker)
lower grid: 1.6fr / 1fr split — main table panel (left), stacked smaller panels (right)

layout.responsiveNote: On narrow viewports, collapse kpiRow to 2 columns, lowerGrid to 1 column, and sidebar to an icon-only or off-canvas drawer.

structure: panel-head: flex row, title block left (panelTitle + panelSubtitle), optional action link right, bottom border separator
panel body: varies by content (pipeline / table / list)
kpi-top: label (left) + icon chip in blue.100 bg with blue.700 icon (right)
kpi-delta: small colored trend line — up=success, down=danger, flat=slate400, using ▲/▼/— glyphs

sidebarNavItem.optionalBadge: pill on the right showing a count, light tint on active state (blue.100 bg / blue.700 text), translucent white on default state

statusPill.shape: fully rounded (20px radius), inline-flex with leading dot
statusPill.rule: Always pair a colored dot/glyph with a text label — never color alone.

pipelineTracker.description: Signature component: horizontal sequence of circular station nodes connected by a line, representing the bakery's fixed production stations in order.
pipelineTracker.connectorLine: 2px solid border between nodes; turns blue.100/blue.500 tint once a station is done
pipelineTracker.labelBelow: station name (body, semibold, ink) + count/status (mono, slate600, or blue.700 if active)

table.header: uppercase, letterspaced, slate400, no background, bottom border on the row
table.row: 13px body text, 1px border bottom, no zebra striping, generous vertical padding (13px)

listRow.description: Used for staff-on-shift and alert lists inside side panels.
listRow.structure: avatar/dot (left) + name/title + subtext (stacked) + trailing status indicator (right, e.g. presence dot or none)
listRow.divider: 1px border bottom between rows, none on last row

topbar.branchSelector: pill-shaped button, white bg, border, status dot (success green) + branch name + chevron
topbar.clock: mono, semibold, blue.700 text on blue.100 chip background
topbar.iconButton: 36x36px white square with border and 9px radius, optional red notification ping dot top-right
topbar.avatar: circular, initials in display font, blue.700/blue.600 background, white text

buttonsAndLinks.panelActionLink: 12px semibold blue.700 text with trailing chevron icon, no background, hover underline optional
buttonsAndLinks.primaryButtonGuideline: Not yet defined in mockup — when added, use blue.600 background, white text, 9px radius, 8-10px vertical padding, matches icon-button sizing conventions

iconography.style: Outline/stroke icons only (stroke-width 2), no filled icons except the brand mark.
iconography.color: Inherits from parent context — slate/blue per component rules above, never multi-color icons

spacingAndRadius.borderRadiusPill: 20px / fully rounded for status pills and the branch selector
spacingAndRadius.panelInnerPadding: 16-20px horizontal, 14-16px vertical for headers; 13px vertical per table/list row

interactionPatterns.hoverStates: Subtle background tint shift only (e.g. rgba(255,255,255,0.06) on dark sidebar, light blue tint on white surfaces). No scale/transform effects.
interactionPatterns.activeStates: Solid fill (white on dark sidebar, blue.600 on pipeline nodes) signals current/selected state.
interactionPatterns.emptyStates: Not yet defined — when needed, follow product voice: explain what's missing and the action to take, no filler copy.
interactionPatterns.motion: Minimal. This system favors instant, low-latency feeling state changes over animated transitions, consistent with a POS/register tool used by floor staff.

voiceAndContent.tone: Plain, operational, no marketing language. Labels name what the user controls (e.g. 'Orders Today', 'Active Stations') not abstract metrics jargon.
voiceAndContent.numberFormatting: Use mono font for all literal numbers/IDs/timestamps so they read distinctly from labels.
voiceAndContent.statusWording: Use short, concrete states: Queued, In progress, Ready, Delayed, Idle, Done — avoid synonyms or inconsistent terms across pages.

knownEntities.note: When generating new pages, reuse these exact names for consistency across the system.

aiUsageInstructions: When asked to build a new page or component for this system, apply colorPalette, typography, layout, and components sections exactly as defined here — do not introduce new colors, fonts, or radii.
Reuse existing component patterns (kpiCard, panel, statusPill, table, listRow, pipelineTracker) rather than inventing new visual patterns for the same kind of content.
Keep the sidebar and topbar structure identical across all pages; only the main content area changes per page.
If a new status type is needed, extend statusPill.variants using the existing 4-color status logic (success/warning/danger/neutral) rather than adding a new hue.
If new entity names (branches, stations, roles) are introduced, add them to knownEntities and reuse consistently.
